latest.title: Launch of New Marketing Program for Detergents
latest.content:
27 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7800F
Itaconix PLC
27 May 2026
For release on RNS Reach: May 27, 2026
Itaconix plc
("Itaconix", the "Company" or the "Group")
Launch of New Marketing Program for Next Generation Consumer Detergents
Itaconix plc (AIM: ITX) (OTCQB: ITXXF), a leading innovator in high-performance plant-based specialty polymers used in consumer products, today announces its new deeperclean.com marketing initiative aimed at North American consumer detergent brand owners.
Deeperclean.com is a North American ecommerce site that offers brand managers ready access to the commercial potential of next generation detergent products enabled by Itaconix® technologies. The Company currently promotes dishwashing and laundry detergent formulations through its SPARX™ Formulated Solution programme. Management's assessment of this programme's success identified opportunities to further accelerate the adoption of new formulations. Interested brand managers can easily order and receive fully packaged detergents with the certainty of performance, marketing claims, and pricing needed to speed product evaluation and purchasing decisions.
The flagship product on deeperclean.com is the 8-gram plant-based dishwashing detergent tablet that Itaconix introduced at the American Cleaning Institute's Innovation Showcase in February.
Although the site is intended primarily for brand managers, the Company expects some retail sales that will generate valuable consumer data to use with brand managers and to assist with future product development.
All orders are handled via a third-party logistics company in the US. Fulfillment to retail customers in the UK is available but will not reflect competitive cost potential due to high shipping charges.
John R. Shaw, CEO of Itaconix, commented:
"Our ingredients are enabling new dishwashing and laundry detergents with improved safety, performance, affordability, and sustainability. We see deeperclean.com as new, more tangible and effective way for brand managers to experience the commercial potential of products enabled by Itaconix technologies. By making it easier to evaluate fully formulated products with proven performance and positioning, we believe this initiative can help accelerate the adoption of next generation detergents. I look forward to reporting on the progress and growth of deeperclean.com over the next few years."
- Ends -
Enquiries:
Itaconix plc
+1 603 775 4400
John R. Shaw / Laura Denner
Rosewood
+44 (0) 207 653 8704
Financial PR
John West / Llew Angus
Canaccord Genuity
+44 (0) 7523 8000
Nominated Adviser and Broker
Adam James / Harry Pardoe
About Itaconix
Itaconix uses its proprietary plant-based polymer technology platform to produce and sell specialty ingredients that improve the safety, performance, and sustainability of consumer products. The Company's current ingredients are enabling and leading new generations of products in detergents, hygiene, and hair care. Itaconix's products contribute to the global low carbon economy.
www.itaconix.com

latest.title: Adjournment of AGM
latest.content:
14 May 2026 17:55
RNS Number : 4243E
Itaconix PLC
14 May 2026
For immediate release
ITACONIX PLC
("Itaconix" or the "Company")
Adjournment of Annual General Meeting
Itaconix plc (AIM: ITX) (OTCQB: ITXXF), a leading innovator in high-performance plant-based specialty polymers, announces that the Annual General Meeting ("AGM"), originally scheduled for earlier today, Thursday 14 May 2026, has been adjourned to 14:00 BST on 10 June 2026 at Fieldfisher LLP, 9th Floor, Riverbank House, 2 Swan Lane, London EC4R 3TT, United Kingdom. Accordingly, no business was conducted and none of the resolutions were considered.
Shareholders are referred to the Notes attached to the Notice of General Meeting, which is available on the Company's website (www.itaconix.com) and which were posted to shareholders on 24 March 2026, with regards to revoking an existing and / or submitting a new proxy appointment.
On that basis, the new deadline for submission of proxy appointments to the Company's registrar will be 14:00 BST on 8 June 2026. Proxy appointments already submitted will remain valid for the adjourned General Meeting unless revoked or superseded by a new proxy.
- Ends -
Itaconix plc
John R. Shaw / Laura Denner
+1 603 775-4400
Rosewood
John West / Llewellyn Angus / Lily Pearce
+44 (0)20 7653 8702
Canaccord Genuity - Nominated Adviser and Sole Broker
Adam James / Harry Pardoe
+44 (0) 20 7523 8000
About Itaconix
Itaconix uses its proprietary plant-based polymer technology platform to produce and sell specialty ingredients that improve the safety, performance, and sustainability of consumer products. The Company's current ingredients are enabling and leading new generations of cost-effective, decarbonized consumer products in home and personal care.
www.itaconix.com

Itaconix PLC

Itaconix plc
("Itaconix" or the "Company")
Director/PDMR Shareholdings
Itaconix (AIM: ITX) (OTCQB:ITXXF), a leading innovator in high-performance plant-based specialty polymers
, announces that Peter Nieuwenhuizen, Non-Executive Chair of the Company, purchased 3,400 ordinary shares in the Company on 5 May 2026 at a price of £1.0935 per ordinary share.
Following this purchase, Peter Nieuwenhuizen holds 36,857 ordinary shares in the Company, representing approximately 0.3% per cent. of the Company's issued share capital.

latest.content:
23 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5640B
Itaconix PLC
23 April 2026
For immediate release
Itaconix plc
("Itaconix" or the "Company")
Grants of Options & PDMR dealings
Itaconix (AIM: ITX) (OTCQB: ITXXF), a leading innovator in high-performance plant-based specialty polymers, announces a grant of options to certain PDMRs.
On 21 April 2026, a total of 384,025 options over ordinary shares of £0.50 in the Company ("Options") were granted under the Company's employee share option plan. Of the Options granted, 216,350 Options were granted to certain persons discharging managerial responsibilities ("PDMRs"). The following PDMRs were granted Options:

The Company's employee share option plan has been designed to reward employees of the Company for performance that delivers value for shareholders and incentivise employees to work effectively together to deliver key corporate objectives. The vesting of the 2026-2028 Long Term Incentive Options is based on achievement of revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and total shareholder return targets within the next 5 years and continued employment.
As at today's date, there are a total of 956,302 Options outstanding.

company_30s.what_they_do: ITX is a UK‑based consumer‑goods company that makes household cleaning products.  Its portfolio focuses on plant‑based detergents, eco‑friendly cleaning agents and a line of sustainable odor‑control solutions.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The cleaning‑product market is worth hundreds of billions worldwide, and buyers are increasingly demanding greener options.  ITX’s competitive edge comes from its proprietary plant‑based formulations, a growing e‑commerce presence, and a partnership with a sustainability‑focused supplier that gives it access to a niche segment of eco‑conscious consumers.
company_30s.current_state: ITX is in a growth‑phase build‑out.  It has just launched a new e‑commerce platform and a plant‑based detergent line, but it still has limited sales volume and is likely operating on a tight cash‑burn schedule.  The stock is trading at the very bottom of its recent price range, suggesting that the market has largely overlooked the company.

rns_narrative: ITX is aggressively expanding its product and marketing footprint – a new detergent brand, a dedicated e‑commerce site, and a plant‑based launch that positions the firm in the high‑growth sustainable‑cleaning niche.

The company is also busy with corporate governance matters: directors are adjusting their holdings, the AGM was adjourned, and options have been granted to key personnel, all of which signal that management is trying to align incentives and keep the business on track.
Strategic partnerships are emerging, notably a sustainable odor‑control agreement that could open a new revenue stream and give ITX a foothold in the industrial‑cleaning market.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly building – the community is largely silent, but a few bullish voices flag acquisition potential.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders point to the new plant‑based line and the e‑commerce push as signs that ITX could become a niche leader, especially if a big consumer brand takes notice.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Critics worry that the company still has no solid revenue base, is burning cash, and may struggle to scale its marketing spend without a clear path to profitability.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Engagement remains low; the mood is neutral with a slight tilt toward optimism as product launches generate modest chatter.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for ITX is flat – there is no recent search activity, giving the stock a high surprise factor if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The official launch of the new plant‑based detergent line (expected in the next 4–6 weeks).  A successful rollout could drive a spike in sales and volume.
Release of the first quarterly earnings report that includes revenue figures for the new product – a positive number would be a clear bounce trigger.
Announcement of the sustainable odor‑control partnership, which could add a new revenue stream and attract institutional interest.
A surge in e‑commerce traffic or a notable increase in order volume on the new website – a technical sign of growing demand.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 20–30% jump in daily volume, price touching the recent low support level, and any positive commentary from institutional analysts.  A confirmation of a new revenue source or a clear earnings beat would also strengthen the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a crash‑recovery cycle – the market has likely already priced in the worst case.  A small amount of buying pressure that breaks the low support would signal that capitulation is done and mean reversion can begin.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If ITX is a deep‑crash (70–95% drop) and the company now has a tangible product launch and partnership, the potential upside is large.  A 150% return is realistic if the price recovers to the midpoint of the 52‑week range, based on historical RED‑signal performance of ~160% average gain.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest threat is that the product launches fail to generate traction, cash burn continues, or the company cannot secure further funding.  In that scenario the stock could stay flat or decline further, but the downside is capped at the current low.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current market price (the stock is already near its bottom).  Potential gain could be 1.5–2× that amount, giving a risk‑to‑reward ratio of roughly 1:2 or better.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a volume spike that confirms a break of the recent low, size the position so that a 1‑2% stop‑loss would still keep you in the trade.  Target 1.5× the entry price, and consider scaling out as the price approaches the midpoint of the 52‑week range.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until you see either a 30% volume jump, a positive earnings announcement, or a clear uptick in e‑commerce traffic.  Use a tight stop just below the recent low to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy signals.  The setup remains a contrarian play.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ITX sits at a deep market trough, its new product launch and partnership offer a clear catalyst, and the upside is large while the downside is already capped – a classic contrarian opportunity.
